title: sieve_glob_to_regex allocates per-char strings
description: sieve_glob_to_regex calls fancy_regex::escape(&other.to_string()) for each non-special character, allocating a String per char. fancy_regex::escape accepts &str; a char can be pushed to a stack buffer or formatted inline. Suggested rewrite: use a small [u8; 4] buffer via char::encode_utf8 or push the char to a scratch String allocated once, and pass it to escape: let mut buf = String::new(); buf.push(other); out.push_str(&fancy_regex::escape(&buf)); buf.clear();. This replaces N small heap allocations with one reused buffer.
